body:
MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY ECONOMIC GROWTH AND 
RESILIENCE  
M I N U T E S 
 
Thursday, March 5, 2026, 5:00 pm  
Christine Sinclair Community Centre  
3713 Kensington Avenue  
Burnaby, BC V5B 0A7  
 
PRESENT:  Mayor Mike Hurley, Chair  
 Councillor James Wang, Vice Chair, In the Chair  
 Councillor Richard T. Lee, Member  
 Councillor Maita Santiago , Member  
 David Carley, SustainTech Solutions  
 Tom Dielschneider, Svante Technologies Inc.  
 Carl Funk, Beedie  
 Ron Hyrnuik, Bridge Studios  
 Arjun Kadaleevanam, Innovation and Economic Dev. Professional  
 Isabel Kolic, Heights Merchants Association  
 Jennifer Scott, Vancouver Whitecaps  
 Dr. Vidya Vankayala, British Columbia Institute of Technology  
 Angie Whitfield, Burnaby Board of Trade  
  
CONSULTANTS:  Amanda Fung, Facilitator  
 Jamie Vann Struth, Facilitator  
  
ABSENT:  Brandon Atherley, Royal Bank of Canada  
 John McCarthy, McCarthy Properties  
  
STAFF:  Juli Halliwell, General Manager Corporate Services / Corporate Officer  
 Bob Klimek, Deputy General Manager Finance  
 Ed Kozak, General Manager Planning & Development  
 Karin Hung, Director Strategic Initiatives  
 Rajvir Rao, Director Intergovernmental Relations & Public Affairs  
 Charlene Liew, Planner 3  
 Denise Chak, Administrative Officer 1  
 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes March 5, 2026  
 
 2 1. CALL TO ORDER  
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, called the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic 
Growth and Resilience (the Task Force) meeting to order at 5:32 pm.  
  
2. TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT  
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, respectfully acknowledged that the City of Burnaby is 
located on the unceded territories of the xʷm əθkʷəy̓əm (Musqueam), S ḵwx̱wú7mesh 
(Squamish), s əlilwətaɬ (Tsleil- Waututh), and kʷikʷ əƛ̓əm (Kwikwetlem) Peoples. Each 
Nation has distinct histories and distinct traditional territories that fully or partially encompass the city. The City encourages you to learn more about the Host Nations whose ancestors have occupied and used these lands, inc luding parts of present -day 
Burnaby, for  thousands of years.  
 
3. ADOPTION OF AGENDA 
3.1 Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Agenda 
for March 5, 2026  
  MOVED AND SECONDED THAT the agenda for the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and 
Resilience meeting of March 5, 2026 be adopted, as presented.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY  
 
4. ADOPTION OF MINUTES  
4.1 Minutes of the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience 
Meeting held on February 19, 2026  
MOVED AND SECONDED 
THAT the minutes of the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and 
Resilience meeting held on February 19, 2026, be now adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY  
 
5. WORKSHOP #5 -  INCLUSIVE ECONOMICS  
5.1 Chair 's Address  
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, provided the following opening remarks:  
Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes March 5, 2026  
 
 3 "Over the last several workshops, we’ve been building a shared understanding of 
Burnaby’s economy —our strengths and assets, the external forces we face, and 
what it means to be resilient and future -ready. Tonight’s conversation continues 
to build on that w ork. 
As we look ahead, an important question for Burnaby is not just how we grow, but who benefits from that growth and how our economic development choices must reflect the values of our city. Burnaby is one of the most diverse 
communities in Canada. That diversity is a real strength, but it may also 
challenge us to think differently about economic development.  
Inclusive economics asks us to consider whether our policies, programs, and 
investments are creating opportunity across the city —or whether barriers still 
exist for certain people, businesses, or communities. This isn’t about lowering 
ambition. It’s about making sure growth is broad
-based, sustainable, and aligned 
with Burnaby’s long -term priorities.  
Tonight, we’ll explore how diversity and inclusion may influence how the City 
should engage in economic development —from which sectors we support, to 
how we engage small businesses, to how we attract investment and talent. This 
conversation will help shape the strategic priorities we intend to articulate by the 
end of this workshop. I again encourage you to approach the discussion with openness and a practical, solutions
-focused mindset.  
With that, I’ll turn it over to the facilitation team to guide us through the session."  
 
5.2 Recap of Previous Workshop 
A. Fung, Facilitator, provided a recap of Workshop #4.  
 
5.3 Icebreaker  
A. Fung, Facilitator, led an icebreaker activity where the Task Force Members 
discussed tradeoffs and strategic priorities.  
 
5.4 Guest Lecture and Discussion -  Inclusive Economics  
Jordan Manning, Manager, Economic Advisory, Deloitte, delivered a guest lecture on inclusive economic growth, highlighting the economic benefits of diversity and 
inclusion, and engaged the Task Force members  in a about strategies for 
attracting and retaining talent, supporting underrepresented groups, and leveraging Burnaby's diversity.  
Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes March 5, 2026  
 
 4 *Dr. Vidya Vankayala arrived at 6:17 pm  
5.5 Interactive Discussion and Group Activities  
A. Fung, Facilitator, led a series of interactive discussions focused on indigenous 
communities, visible minorities, newcomers and identifying strategic priorities.  
*Mayor Hurley left the meeting at 7:14 pm and did not return  
 
5.6 Closing Remarks  
In closing, A. Fung, Facilitator,  advised that Deloitte will conduct stakeholder 
interviews with sectors not represented on the Task Force and deploy a survey 
on behalf of the City of Burnaby , in partnership with the Burnaby Board of Trade, 
to validate the top strategic priorities  of the Task Force.  
 The next workshop will be held on April 16, 2026 at 5:00 pm.  
 
6. OTHER BUSINESS  
There was no other business brought before the Task Force at this time.  
 7. ADJOURNMENT  
By unanimous consent and without objection, the Task Force meeting adjourned at 8:10 
pm. 
  
   
Councillor James Wang  
VICE CHAIR   Denise Chak  
ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moving from Strategy to Action on Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, where members will review strategic priorities and move from planning to action. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — has been working to shape Burnaby's economic future, with a focus on inclusive growth that benefits a wide range of businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Attend or follow the April 16 workshop to stay informed on Burnaby's upcoming economic strategy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Task Force Explores Inclusive Economic Growth Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience met on March 5, 2026 to discuss 'inclusive economics' — the idea that city growth should create opportunity for everyone, including small businesses, newcomers, and underrepresented groups. The Task Force is working toward a set of strategic priorities, and Deloitte will soon survey businesses (in partnership with the Burnaby Board of Trade) to help shape those priorities before the next workshop on April 16, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Burnaby Board of Trade survey and fill it out — your input shapes the city's economic strategy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Economic Growth Task Force Workshop #6 — next meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moves from Strategy to Action Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, where members will review strategic priorities and turn them into a concrete action plan. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — has been meeting since early 2026 to shape how Burnaby supports economic growth, small businesses, and underrepresented groups.
briefs.action_required: Attend or follow the April 16 workshop to see how Burnaby's economic strategy may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moves Toward Economic Growth Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, where members will review strategic priorities and move from planning to action. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — has been working to shape a long-term economic strategy for Burnaby that focuses on inclusive growth, meaning making sure all businesses and communities can benefit.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final strategy — it could shape city programs and support available to your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Reviews Economic Growth Strategy Priorities
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to meet on April 16, 2026 to review strategic priorities and move from planning to action. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — has been working with consultants to shape a city-wide economic strategy focused on inclusive growth, meaning making sure all businesses and residents can benefit from Burnaby's economy.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final strategy — it could shape city programs and support available to your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Economic Growth Strategy Findings
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to meet on April 16, 2026 to review engagement findings and move from strategy to action. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — has been gathering input on how Burnaby can grow its economy in a way that benefits everyone, including small businesses, newcomers, and underrepresented groups.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 16 Task Force meeting or follow its outcomes — recommendations could shape future City business programs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth Moves to Strategy Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, shifting from research to action by reviewing strategic priorities and engagement findings. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and community representatives — is working to shape Burnaby's long-term economic development strategy, with a focus on inclusive growth for small businesses and underrepresented communities.
briefs.action_required: Attend or follow the April 16 workshop to see how Burnaby's economic strategy may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moves from Strategy to Action on Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its sixth workshop on April 16, 2026, where members will review strategic priorities and take part in group activities to turn ideas into an action plan. This is an ongoing process — no final decisions have been made yet — but the outcomes will shape how Burnaby supports local businesses, attracts investment, and promotes inclusive economic growth across the city.
briefs.action_required: Attend or follow the April 16 workshop to stay informed on economic priorities that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #6 — Strategy to Action session
